title: SK Hynix loses more than 15% in Seoul after collapse of its ADRs on Nasdaq
summary: The South Korean chipmaker wiped out in a single day all the gains from its U.S. IPO, while analysts see a buying opportunity and others fear the end of the sector's rally.

title: Trump reinstates Iran blockade and oil surges after attacks in the Gulf
summary: The United States reimposes sanctions and Yemen attacks Houthi-controlled airports, raising Brent crude and pressuring gold and silver lower, while global markets react with volatility.

title: Wall Street falls dragged by technology sector and Middle East escalation
summary: The Nasdaq led losses with a decline of more than 1%, hit by poor semiconductor earnings and geopolitical uncertainty, while the Dow Jones and S&P 500 also closed lower.

title: Shell sells its renewable energy subsidiary in India for $1.8 billion
summary: The company transfers Sprng Energy to Aditya Birla Renewables in a transaction that reflects its strategy of focusing on oil and gas, while the buyer expands its solar portfolio.

title: Intel will invest 5 billion euros to expand its chip factory in Ireland
summary: The investment will go towards increasing production capacity for AI processors, amid high demand and global competition.

title: Figma shares soar 11.9% and options volume surges
summary: Shares of the design company rise sharply and the options market records more than 145,000 contracts traded, in a move that surprises analysts.

title: Two articles defend prediction markets as an informational tool
summary: Both a Spanish media outlet and the Financial Times argue that these markets can aggregate knowledge and predict events better than traditional polls, despite regulatory criticism.

title: Bitcoin falls 2.11% on July 13 amid geopolitical tensions
summary: The leading cryptocurrency falls along with other risk assets, reflecting risk aversion after the attacks in Iran and uncertainty about monetary policy.

title: Wall Street expects highest investment banking fee revenue in four years
summary: SpaceX's IPO and major mergers boost bank revenues, which could reach their highest level since 2022.

title: Spain's Ibex 35 falls on rising oil and tensions with Iran
summary: The Spanish stock market closed in the red in a volatile session, hit by rising crude oil and geopolitical uncertainty, though it managed to limit losses.

title: Germany's DAX closes virtually unchanged above 25,000 points
summary: The German index held steady in a mixed trading day, with gains in software and communications offsetting losses in the energy sector.

title: TSMC reports 68% revenue growth and investors warn of Asian chip dominance
summary: The world's largest chipmaker beats expectations, while a Financial Times article warns of the growing weight of Asian semiconductors in stock indices.

title: Japan successfully tests reusable rocket and high-speed flying car
summary: SkyDrive conducts a flight off the coast of Yamaguchi and a rocket prototype lands in a controlled manner, moving toward commercialization by 2028.

title: California sues to block Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery merger worth $110 billion
summary: A group of states led by California files an antitrust lawsuit to block the megamerger, arguing that it would harm competition and consumers.

title: Meta invests more than $50 billion in a 5-gigawatt data center in Louisiana
summary: Meta's AI infrastructure expansion continues with one of the world's largest data centers, reinforcing its computing capacity.

title: EU buys record amount of gas from Russia's Yamal plant despite sanctions
summary: European imports of LNG from Russia's flagship plant hit record highs, drawing criticism over energy dependence.

title: MUFG becomes Japan's most valuable company for the first time in four decades
summary: Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group overtakes Toyota in market capitalization, fueled by higher interest rates and a strong financial sector.

title: Fed doesn't rule out rate hikes if core inflation picks up, says Waller
summary: Fed Governor Waller warns that monetary policy must be ready to respond to potential inflationary pressures amid uncertainty.

title: Deutsche Bank, HVB and Commerzbank sue Linde for more than 800 million over Russian project
summary: The German banks seek compensation for the collapse of an energy project in Russia after international sanctions, in one of the year's largest lawsuits.

title: Lockheed Martin and RTX to quadruple production of interceptor missiles for US Army
summary: The capacity expansion seeks to respond to growing aerial threats, though the weakness of the U.S. manufacturing sector presents an obstacle.
